channel.title: Digg AI Feed from X
channel.description: Top 10 AI stories from Digg's X-ranked tech feed with click-through links to original source content.

item.title: Anthropic pulls Claude Fable 5 offline one day after redeployment following a successful jailbreak by security researcher Pliny
item.description: The model was redeployed following discussions with the US government.
item.content:encoded:
The model was redeployed following discussions with the US government.

Original source: https://www.anthropic.com/news/redeploying-fable-5

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/x9l7qu4s

Anthropic restored global access to Claude Fable 5 after the US Department of Commerce lifted export controls, adding fresh classifiers to curb cybersecurity misuse and temporarily routing routine coding queries to Opus 4.8 while the safeguards improve. 18 51 4 5 5.5K CH #1364 | @KIMMONISMUS LA #1218 | @SCALING01 J⧉ #674 | @REPLIGATE JS #576 | @MONONOFU B( #530 | @BEFFJEZOS ND #22 | @NANDODF

  • Views: 5,520
  • Likes: 51
  • Bookmarks: 5
  • Replies: 18
  • Posts: 9

Top authors

  • Nando de Freitas
  • Beff (e/acc)
  • Julian Schrittwieser
  • j⧉nus
  • Lisan al Gaib
I’m stoked that Fable is available again! This is the first model where I went from individually reviewing changes to just reviewing PRs, it’s astonishingly smart - it’s when I really felt in my bones that coding will be solved by end of year

item.title: Founder @levelsio warns the EU's new sub-€150 customs duty will trigger severe shipping delays and complex tax burdens
item.description: Recipients must manually classify imports using complex tariff codes.
item.content:encoded:
Recipients must manually classify imports using complex tariff codes.

Original source: https://x.com/vonderleyen/status/2072222371461705796

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/3v4ejtj6

The EU ends its €150 de minimis exemption on 1 July 2026, replacing it with a temporary €3 flat customs fee per tariff line on low-value distance sales until mid-2028, after which normal product-specific tariffs kick in. A Portugal-based founder has flagged potential weeks-long delays, broken portals, and confusing classifications for recipients, yet official guidance stresses a simplified transitional regime handled mainly by carriers and platforms. 2.8K 2.2K 299 489 594.1K @L #627 | @LEVELSIO RP #395 | @TYPESFAST

  • Views: 6,367
  • Likes: 94
  • Bookmarks: 9
  • Quotes: 3
  • Replies: 13
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Ryan Petersen
  • @levelsio
$3b

item.title: Meta is reportedly developing a cloud service to rent its surplus AI GPU capacity to external customers
item.description: The move aims to offset massive AI infrastructure costs.
item.content:encoded:
The move aims to offset massive AI infrastructure costs.

Original source: https://x.com/wallstengine/status/2072298205379977548

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/gypsshy5

  • Views: 20,790
  • Likes: 117
  • Bookmarks: 5
  • Quotes: 1
  • Replies: 13
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • Lisan al Gaib
  • Zephyr
Bad news for neos & other hyperscalers

item.title: US House advances the KIDS Act to regulate AI chatbots and mandate web age verification
item.description: Critics warn the rules could force universal web identity verification.
item.content:encoded:
Critics warn the rules could force universal web identity verification.

Original source: https://clerk.house.gov/evs/2026/roll228.xml

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/s32xoq12

The House cleared a wide-ranging package that layers fresh disclosure and safety rules onto AI chatbots while adding age checks for adult content sites, sending the measure to the Senate after a 267-117 vote. 65 908 216 42 21.2K DJ #1711 | @DAN_JEFFRIES1 T( #501 | @TEORTAXESTEX

  • Views: 659
  • Likes: 7
  • Replies: 5
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • Daniel Jeffries
Here’s the roll call: https://clerk.house.gov/evs/2026/roll228.xml

item.title: Google DeepMind's Pranav Shyam argues short-horizon PPO reduces to bandit problems, but open-source developer Grad disputes the latency impact
item.description: Grad says 20,000-token reasoning workloads bypass critic bottlenecks.
item.content:encoded:
Grad says 20,000-token reasoning workloads bypass critic bottlenecks.

Original source: https://x.com/recurseparadox/status/2072232321873539132

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/l5lv32c1

A public exchange on X between Google DeepMind researcher Pranav Shyam and open-source account Grad highlights how short-horizon reinforcement learning setups mirror bandit problems, removing any advantage from value functions while still forcing PPO to wait on its critic and add latency. 8 6 0 2 135 PS #1597 | @RECURSEPARADOX GR #1525 | @GRAD62304977

  • Views: 135
  • Likes: 6
  • Bookmarks: 2
  • Replies: 8
  • Posts: 11

Top authors

  • Grad
  • Pranav Shyam
Like my point is for long horizon I would trust the value models less and less and with compute scaling I would prefer a bigger group size or other ideas to improve credit assignment here If we assume the value model is pretty good then surely it should’ve always been what we should’ve been doing and save lots of compute still

item.title: AI chip startup Etched raises $800 million and tapes out its chip after deploying an emergency sprint to Bangalore
item.description: The company has secured over $1 billion in customer contracts.
item.content:encoded:
The company has secured over $1 billion in customer contracts.

Original source: https://x.com/InvestLikeBest/status/2072296999186026720

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/zdtxg01y

Etched has raised a total of $800 million across rounds to develop purpose-built inference chips for transformer models, with the latest tranche closing at a $5 billion valuation and the company now validating its first taped-out A0 silicon ahead of rack shipments this summer. 11 131 21 50 23.7K SC #827 | @BLADER PO #571 | @PATRICK_OSHAG

  • Views: 1,076
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Patrick OShaughnessy
  • Siqi Chen
@Tijori1 They are literally saying that the Bangalore team (vendor) was putting them behind schedule and that's why they had to stay here. That's not a pat on the back.

item.title: Technical commentators joke that a highly recursive post by Paul Graham resembles Lisp without parentheses
item.description: Commentators joked the nested sentence structure needed closing parentheses
item.content:encoded:
Commentators joked the nested sentence structure needed closing parentheses

Original source: https://x.com/sytelus/status/2072242266413179055

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/diwv9rlk

Paul Graham posted a single sentence describing Y Combinator that folds the familiar "make something people want" phrasing back on itself, creating an extra layer of self-reference that immediately prompted jokes about missing Lisp parentheses. 1 7 0 0 539 SS #1474 | @SYTELUS BS #797 | @BSCHOLL

  • Views: 539
  • Likes: 7
  • Replies: 1
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Blake Scholl 🛫
  • Shital Shah
@paulg Needs more parentheses

item.title: Prime Intellect's kalomaze argues GANs remain ubiquitous because they power the autoencoders used in modern diffusion models
item.description: The debate counters claims that the technology is obsolete.
item.content:encoded:
The debate counters claims that the technology is obsolete.

Original source: https://x.com/kalomaze/status/2072229341791134096

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/oju244es

In a July 1 X reply, Prime Intellect researcher kalomaze pushes back on the idea that GANs have faded by pointing to their continued presence inside autoencoders that support latent diffusion pipelines, treating that integration as evidence of ongoing relevance rather than any fall from grace. 1 3 0 0 235 KA #1216 | @KALOMAZE GA #598 | @GABRIEL1

  • Views: 235
  • Likes: 3
  • Replies: 1
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • gabriel
  • kalomaze
@willdepue GANs are hella satisfying

item.title: Elie Bakouch says Claude's safety filters blocked a routine request to retrieve an Artifact link from a past session
item.description: The filter flagged the cross-session request as a policy violation
item.content:encoded:
The filter flagged the cross-session request as a policy violation

Original source: https://x.com/xeophon/status/2072216410558779475

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/mr6ehd82

A research engineer prepping slides for a talk on automated AI research hit an immediate API error when asking Claude to fetch a prior-session Artifact link for simple plot color swaps, with the model citing a usage policy breach despite the request involving nothing more than theme adjustments like blue for Claude and orange for Codex. 1 2 0 0 100 FB #1782 | @XEOPHON EL #1138 | @ELIEBAKOUCH

  • Views: 100
  • Likes: 2
  • Replies: 1
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • elie
  • Florian Brand
@xeophon you know the worst part, the color was like blue for claude and orange for codex and just asked in another convo to switch it and send the artifact link 😭

item.title: Google DeepMind's Pranav Shyam defines short context horizons at 32k tokens or fewer and long context at 64k or more
item.description: Longer horizons require complex multi-round tool calls and code execution.
item.content:encoded:
Longer horizons require complex multi-round tool calls and code execution.

Original source: https://x.com/recurseparadox/status/2072240314035970240

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/1vg8ir9u

Google DeepMind researcher Pranav Shyam drew a line in an X thread between simpler AI interactions capped at 32K tokens and the more demanding setups that kick in at 64K or 128K, where models must juggle repeated tool calls and code execution to keep state updated across steps. 1 2 0 0 18 PS #1597 | @RECURSEPARADOX GR #1525 | @GRAD62304977

  • Views: 18
  • Likes: 2
  • Replies: 1
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Grad
  • Pranav Shyam
@recurseparadox but whats the intuition here that tool calls and environments have a big effect here. As in its a clear point the value model can make a more accurate prediction on the final expected reward? Why is it different to say a long reasoning chain with a clear step made?
